author: workflowcat
subject: fix: MSA reviewed-mode layout — expand page grid, simpler selectors
body:
The contract page's "reviewed" view (body + floated voice comments)
was clipping under the default 3-column page grid. Default grid gave
the body column `minmax(0, var(--measure))` ≈ 813px, but reviewed
mode wanted 62rem (992px) for text + voice gutter. Result: voices
squashed, content overlapping voices, brittle absolute-positioned
vertical rule at hardcoded `left: 41.5rem`.

Fixes:

- layout.css: at ≥1280px, `:has()` the contract-layout[data-view="reviewed"]
  to expand the page grid to `13rem + 62rem` and hide the TOC margin
  (briefing blockquote serves as the TOC). Also hide margin in brief mode
  at ≥1024px. Add a max-width exception for `article:has(.contract-layout)`
  so the defensive `> * { max-width: 100% }` rule doesn't clip it.

- [id].astro: raise two-column breakpoint to 1280px (below that, voices
  render inline like mobile). Replace the per-tag max-width list with
  `> *:not(.voice) { max-width: calc(100% - 20rem) }` — responsive to
  container. Drop the absolute `::before` vertical rule. Add `clear: right`
  on h2 so each section starts below its preceding floats. Refactor brief
  mode to "hide all direct children, opt back in" for robustness.

Verified at 1440px (content 652 / voice 342 / 38px gap, zero overlaps)
and at 1200px (voices fall back to inline full-width).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: three-layer view on contracts — clean / reviewed / brief
body:
Contract pages now support three distinct reading modes via a toggle
at the top of each contract:

  1. Чистий текст — just the contract, voices hidden, text reads
     as a clean legal document. Use this when preparing for signature
     or sharing with the client.

  2. З коментарями (default) — contract in a narrow left column,
     voices float into a dedicated right column with a thin vertical
     rule separating them. True spatial separation, no inline
     interruptions. Each voice sits next to the clause it comments on.

  3. Лише бриф — contract body hidden, only the negotiation brief
     + section headers + all voices remain. The cheat sheet view:
     all commentary without the legal text.

View choice persists in localStorage per browser.

Implementation:
- View toggle UI in contract header (3-button group)
- data-view attribute on contract-layout wrapper controls CSS
- Reviewed mode: body widens to 62rem, text stays 40rem, voices
  float right at 18rem, vertical rule at 41.5rem
- Clean mode: voices + top brief hidden via display:none
- Brief mode: paragraphs/clauses/tables hidden, headers + voices kept

Also removed the preview dialog (200+ lines of JS/CSS) — it was
unreachable after we removed the export buttons.

Lint: 96 entries, 0 warnings, 0 errors. Build: 81 pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: Voice component — Disco Elysium margin comments on contracts
body:
Replaces blockquote annotations with a proper <Voice> component that
renders as margin comments from different organizational perspectives.

Component (src/components/Voice.astro):
- 5 voices with distinct colors and fixed sides:
  ⚖️ Юридичний (steel blue, left) — legal risk, regulatory context
  💼 Бізнес (muted green, right) — revenue, deal dynamics, cash flow
  🎯 Переговори (lavender, right) — what to say, trade-offs
  🔧 Delivery (warm amber, left) — practical workability, team impact
  🔗 Взаємодія (neutral, full-width) — clause connections

Layout behavior:
- Mobile: full-width inline cards with colored left/right border
- Desktop (768px+): half-width cards aligned left or right within
  body column — creates a dialogue/conversation feel
- Wide desktop (1440px+): floats into actual page margins via
  negative margins, like Tufte-style sidenotes

Applied to all 5 contract templates:
- MSA: 21 voices (UK variant converted from blockquotes)
- T&M: 29 voices
- Dedicated Team: 30 voices
- SoW: 38 voices
- DPA: 37 voices
Total: 155 margin comments across the contract library.

Also stripped export buttons from contract page header (previously
planned cut — removed .docx, .md, .zip download links and preview
button; kept language toggle and "ready to sign" pill).

Lint: 96 entries, 0 warnings, 0 errors. Build: 81 pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: multi-perspective negotiation annotations on all 5 contracts
body:
Adds practical negotiation guidance to every contract template, written
from four organizational perspectives:

  ⚖️ Юридичний — legal risk, regulatory context, clause mechanics
  💼 Бізнес — revenue impact, deal velocity, cash flow dynamics
  🎯 Переговори — what to say to the client, trade-off positioning
  🔧 Delivery — practical workability, how it affects the team
  🔗 Взаємодія — how clauses depend on and connect to each other

Each contract has a negotiation brief at the top identifying the 2-3
hottest clauses and key trade-offs, plus role-specific annotations
between every major section.

Contracts annotated:
- MSA (UK variant refactored with 4-perspective pattern)
- T&M (rate increase, budget caps, time tracking, overtime)
- Dedicated Team (replacement SLA, ramp-down, vacancy cost, bench)
- SoW (scope definition, change orders, holdback, acceptance)
- DPA (sub-processors, audit rights, breach notification, deletion)

All annotations in Ukrainian. Research-backed negotiation dynamics from
Norton Rose Fulbright, DLA Piper, IAOP patterns.

Lint: 96 entries, 0 warnings, 0 errors. Build: 81 pages, 11K words.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: add negotiation briefs + clause interaction notes to MSA
body:
Embeds practical negotiation guidance directly into the MSA contract
template (both EN and UK variants). Three types of annotations:

1. **Negotiation brief** at the top — which 3 of 9 sections get
   negotiated in every deal, key trade-offs to know before reading.

2. **Interaction notes** between clauses — how IP depends on payment,
   how payment-dispute and late-interest form a two-layer system,
   how termination/IP/payment form a sequential chain.

3. **Negotiation notes** per clause — what clients typically redline,
   what to concede vs hold, common trade-off patterns.

Research-backed (Norton Rose Fulbright, DLA Piper, IAOP patterns):
- Liability cap is the most contested clause ("always last to close")
- IP ownership and payment terms are the other two hot spots
- Key trade-offs: carve-outs ↔ cap multiple, IP assignment ↔ retained
  license, payment cycle ↔ late payment interest
- Must-hold: mutual liability, 12-month floor, indemnities inside cap
- Tradeable: payment cycle, non-compete scope, dispute forum

Annotations use blockquote styling (>) with emoji markers:
🔍 = negotiation note, 🔗 = clause interaction, ⚠️ = critical item

Lint: 96 entries, 0 warnings, 0 errors. Build: 81 pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: rewrite home as Ukrainian engagement lifecycle guide
body:
Complete rewrite of the home page from a contract catalog to a
step-by-step engagement playbook:

  1. NDA — перший документ
  2. MSA — основний договір
  3. Модель ціноутворення (T&M / Dedicated Team / SoW)
  4. DPA — якщо є персональні дані
  5. SLA — операційний додаток

Each stage includes:
- Ukrainian explanation of what the document is and when to sign it
- Links to the template and key clauses
- "Типові запитання" (common pushback) with expandable Q&A:
  what clients ask and what to say back, with links to relevant
  cases that show the clause in action

Also reverts all chrome to Ukrainian (nav, search, breadcrumbs,
backlinks, cast labels, mobile nav, command palette) — the audience
is Ukrainian account managers and sales people.

The pricing stage uses a 3-card grid comparing T&M / Dedicated Team /
Fixed Price (SoW) with "when to use" guidance for each model.

Cases and clauses remain as reference sections at the bottom,
but the primary reading experience is the lifecycle guide.

Lint: 96 entries, 0 warnings, 0 errors. Build: 81 pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: workflowcat
subject: refactor: focus wiki as core product, demote side-projects to /tools/
body:
Major simplification pass to make the site lighter, quicker, and
easier on the eyes. Follows the redesign plan in mighty-floating-honey.

Cuts:
- Deleted /documents/* routes (middle layer between clauses and contracts)
- Deleted /changelog route (dev tool, not reader destination)
- Removed ScrollProgress, ReadingTools from layout
- Removed wikilink popover tooltip (90+ lines of JS + CSS)
- Removed ExportMenu from clause and workflow pages
- Removed RecentlyViewed from sidebar
- Trimmed rail nav from 12 items to 5 (Home, Contracts, Clauses, Cases, Cast)
- Removed secondary nav section entirely

Language:
- All chrome now English (nav, search, breadcrumbs, backlinks labels,
  cast metadata, case workflow cards, meta description)
- Default html lang switched from uk to en
- Content stays as-authored (mostly Ukrainian prose)

Visual tone-down:
- Background: cream (#f8f3e1) → white (#ffffff)
- Removed all text-transform: uppercase from chrome
- Removed letter-spacing from labels and buttons (tracking → 0)
- Removed offset box-shadows from CommandPalette
- Rules and borders lightened (#e2d9bf → #e5e5e5)

Home page:
- Rewrote intro from user perspective, not developer perspective
- "Grab a contract template", "Understand why each clause is written",
  "Learn from what went wrong before" — action-oriented, not architecture

Result: 81 pages (down from 93), cleaner nav, lighter aesthetic.
Lint: 96 entries, 0 warnings, 0 errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: anonymize contracts, add T&M/DedTeam/SoW/DPA templates, rewrite home
body:

Anonymization:

  terms, roles, cast). Zero references to "Sloboda" remain in src/.
- Renamed services-agreement-sloboda-2026 → services-agreement-2026

  wikilinks + aliases throughout).

New clauses (6, English only):
- milestone-payment: fixed-price milestone billing with 10% holdback
- acceptance-criteria: 5-day review, deemed acceptance, cure period
- change-order: scope freeze + formal change order protocol
- data-processing: GDPR Article 28 processor obligations
- dedicated-team-terms: retainer, ramp-up/down, replacement guarantee
- expense-reimbursement: pre-approved expenses, €500 threshold

New contract templates (4, English only):
- time-and-materials-2026: hourly billing, time reports, expense clause
- dedicated-team-2026: monthly retainer, headcount commitment, 50%
  vacancy reduction, 15-day replacement SLA
- statement-of-work-2026: fixed-price milestones, acceptance criteria,
  change order process, scope freeze
- data-processing-agreement-2026: GDPR DPA with annexes for data
  categories, sub-processors, and technical measures

New document wiki pages (4) with Ukrainian prose descriptions,
comparison tables, and cross-links between agreement types.

Home page:
- Added "How it works" section explaining clauses → contracts →
  cases → cast composition model in English
- Added "Contract Templates" section listing all contracts
- Switched all divider labels and "About" section to English
- Kept WeatherReport and content lists

Lint: 96 entries, 0 warnings, 0 errors. Build: 92 pages.

author: workflowcat

First run of the new precompute script after committing the fix —
regenerates so the deployed changelog shows its own fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: workflowcat
subject: fix: precompute git history to JSON so Vercel builds work
body:
readGitLog() was shelling out to git log inside .astro pages via
execSync. That works locally but fails on Vercel, which checks out
the source without a .git directory at build time. Result: the home
page's WeatherReport and the /changelog page silently rendered with
no git history.

Fix is a two-step decoupling:

  1. scripts/build-git-log.mjs runs during prebuild, captures up to
     100 commits from the local .git into src/data/git-history.json.
     Tolerant of git being unavailable — if git log fails and a
     snapshot already exists, the script keeps it.

  2. src/lib/git-log.ts now imports the JSON snapshot and slices it,
     instead of shelling out. Same exported API (readGitLog,
     affectedContent), no caller changes needed.

The committed JSON is "state of the world at the developer's last
local build". On Vercel, prebuild's git log call no-ops and the
committed JSON is what gets rendered. That means the site is
effectively one commit behind the very latest push (whatever commit
regenerates the JSON isn't in the JSON yet), which is acceptable
for a changelog.

Also fixes a parser bug in the original git log logic: `--name-only`
outputs file names *between* pretty-format chunks because %x1e is
inside the pretty block, so files land after the terminator, not
inside it. The new script uses two passes (meta + files-by-hash)
with distinct separators.

Verified: 11 commits parsed, 78 pages built, changelog shows
"11 останніх комітів".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: five cross-character cases closing story-thread backlog
body:
Five 2026 cases that pull multiple cast members into the same plot and
turn previously-flagged "(backlog)" story threads into concrete precedents.

- horizon-phase-2-precedent-2026: Horizon's new HoE tries to invoke the
  2025 SLA handover addendum as binding precedent for a 3x-larger Phase 2.
  We refuse, explain why context changes everything, rewrite the addendum.
- aria-second-project-payment-leverage-2026: Aria returns with a 5x project
  and we use the moment to renegotiate payment terms via a side-letter —
  leverage without punishment, institutional memory via Jordi.
- client-to-client-poach-non-solicit-2026: Horizon's new HoE asks whether
  our non-solicit can "get Lena back" from Kestrel. Answer is no, for
  chronology and scope reasons. Audit finds one AM misstep; we tighten
  cross-client reference rules.
- cascading-precedent-helvetic-reference-2026: Dutch insurtech prospect
  arrives with Helvetic's exact cap language copy-pasted into their
  redline kit. We calibrate a new €75k cap to their actual project size
  and add a "Helvetic-cap detector" to the intake checklist.
- lead-attribution-atelier-brun-spinoff-2026: Élise spins off Papeterie
  Brun; Maya claims referral. We finally write the three-tier Lead
  Attribution addendum (full/partial/none + disclosure requirement) and
  field-test it by paying Maya 50%.

Each case links forward from the relevant cast profiles; seven cast files
updated to replace "(backlog)" placeholders with concrete wikilinks.

Lint: 82 entries, 0 warnings, 0 errors. Build: 78 pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: workflowcat
subject: docs: tool-splitting design note
body:
Captures the thinking around whether to split Drafters into
separate wiki / builder / heatmaps tools. Lays out four options,
recommends the plugin/integration model (Option 3) as the
pragmatic next step, and enumerates concrete migration phases
if the split is committed to.

Key point: the three tools have genuinely different personas
and rhythms, but the cross-linking that makes Drafters feel
like one product is worth protecting. Don't split into separate
sites prematurely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: recurring cast, case studies, anonymized contracts
body:
Adds a "cast" content collection of recurring fake characters
(companies + people) used across case studies, scrubs real
customer data from all contracts/clauses/documents, expands the
case-study library, and wires the cast into navigation, backlinks,
and the command palette.

Cast collection

Case studies

  [[cast/id]] wikilinks woven through prose

Anonymization
- Replaced HRB260696B → HRB123456X
- Replaced DE366220301 → DE999888777
- Replaced Am Märchenbrunnen 7 → Musterstraße 1
- Replaced Olga Iakubovska → Jana Weber
- Replaced "— Olga" commentary sign-offs with role labels
- Stripped source:/driveSource: Google Docs URLs from frontmatter

  path references

Infrastructure

- content-index.mjs: COLLECTION_TYPES + ROUTE_PREFIX include cast

- collection-helpers.ts: cast labels (uk: Персонажі, en: Cast)

Build: 77 entries scanned, 0 warnings, 0 errors. 73 pages built.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: phase 3 — reading UX, analytics, contract builder, content linter
body:
The biggest single session so far. Five phases of improvements
organized into one commit. Walkthroughs killed. Reading tools,
analytics, delight, and hardening all shipped.

## Phase A — reading UX

- Walkthrough mode deleted (cleaned up dead weight)
- Focus mode: press `f` to hide rail, margin, backlinks,
  footers — pure reading view
- Read aloud: press `a` to have the browser speak the current
  page via Web Speech API
- Copy as markdown: press `C` to copy the current page as
  clean markdown to clipboard
- Highlight-to-annotate: select any text, click "+ нотатка",
  annotations saved to localStorage, panel opens with `.`
- Paragraph-level anchors: every top-level paragraph gets an id
  "p-N" via a custom remark plugin, click the ¶ marker at
  paragraph's left edge to copy a deep-link
- Reading-time stamps on every content page
- /compare page for side-by-side clause comparison
- Recently-viewed widget in the nav rail
- Floating reading-tools toolbar bottom-right

## Phase B — better exports

- New md-to-docx walker in scripts/lib/md-to-docx.mjs: handles
  pipe tables properly (with header row, separator, body),
  numbered lists, bullet lists, blockquotes, bold/italic/code,
  horizontal rules
- Wikilink brackets are now cleanly flattened to their display
  text (no more leaking [[Target|display]] into docx output)
- Escape sequences \_ are unescaped properly
- Contract docx exports get a cover page with title + parties,
  running header with contract title, footer with 'Drafters ·
  page N / total'
- Clause commentary sections are stripped from contract
  inlines by heuristic regex

## Contract builder (/builder)

- Client-side clause picker with full library loaded from
  /clauses.json manifest generated at build time
- Filter chips by tag, search input, language toggle
- Click library items to add, arrows to reorder, × to remove
- Live markdown preview in the right column
- Download as .md (client-side Blob + download)
- URL hash encodes selection for shareable links
- Preset links to all 5 existing contracts
- Falls back to existing contracts for .docx (Vercel edge
  function for custom .docx is future work)

## Phase C — patterns made visible

- /clauses/heat — heat map grid of all clauses, each cell sized
  and colored by usage count, with metrics for cross-pillar
  reuse, orphans, average reuse, most-reused
- /analytics — Jaccard similarity matrix between contracts,
  cross-pillar clause list, usage distribution chart,
  metric cards
- /graph — edges now weighted by usage count (thicker lines
  for more-used clauses), nodes sized by weight

## Phase D — delight

- Weather report on homepage: live counts of clauses /
  contracts / workflows / cross-pillar reuse / commits this
  week / orphans / drafts
- Retro mode: visit any page with ?theme=retro for a 1970s
  typewriter aesthetic (Courier, yellowed paper, purple
  carbon-copy accents). Persists in localStorage.
- Export animation: clicking the .docx button on a contract
  page briefly highlights every section/clause in sequence
  so you see what's being bundled
- Margin notes populated on 4 key clauses: ip-assignment,
  late-payments-interest, liability-limitation-mutual,
  non-compete. Each has a handwritten-style "Legal" note
  with real editorial commentary.

## Phase E — hardening + growth

- 4 new reusable clauses added earlier this session:
  termination-subcontract, termination-at-will,
  liability-limitation-mutual, effect-of-termination.
  All cross-pillar. All in uk+en.
- pnpm new:clause <baseId> "Title UA" "Title EN" —
  scaffolds uk + en clause files from template
- pnpm new:contract <newBaseId> --based-on <existing> —
  clones a contract to a new variant
- pnpm lint:content — content linter using gray-matter,
  catches broken wikilinks, orphan clauses, missing
  updated timestamps, active contracts missing essential
  clauses (dispute-resolution-uk, liability-limitation-mutual,
  effect-of-termination), expired reviews
- CONTRIBUTING.md with the full authoring guide
- Services Agreement composedOf updated with the full
  protection quartet so the audit passes

## Content updates

- 4 new clause baseIds × 2 languages = 8 new clause files
- 10 compiled contract bodies now using the new termination
  and liability clauses
- Sidenote editorial commentary on 4 key legal clauses

## Stats

- Pages:     57 → 57 (stable — walkthroughs gone,
             heat/analytics/builder/compare added)
- Clauses:   15 → 19 baseIds (38 variant files)
- Cross-pillar clauses: 3 (dispute-resolution-uk,
             liability-limitation-mutual,
             effect-of-termination)
- Pagefind words: 3556 → 3637
- Lint status: 0 warnings / 0 errors

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: contracts collection — compiled, signable contract exports
body:
Split 'documents' (wiki pages with commentary) from 'contracts'
(actual signable contract text). Contracts are composed from clauses
via <Clause baseId="X" lang="Y"/> inlines and exported as full
.docx/.md/.zip ready to print and sign.

New clauses (4 baseIds × uk+en = 8 files)
- termination-subcontract — 3-month probation + 14-day standard
- termination-at-will — MSA variant, 30-day then 14-day notice
- liability-limitation-mutual — cross-pillar shared, mutual
  indemnification + max liability = 12 months fees
- effect-of-termination — cross-pillar shared, payment settlement
  + handover + survival of IP/confidentiality/non-solicit

All four subcontracts and the MSA now reference
`liability-limitation-mutual`, `effect-of-termination` and
`dispute-resolution-uk` — three genuine cross-pillar clauses.

New contracts collection
- Zod-typed, each entry has id, baseId, documentId, lang, title,
  contractor, counterparty
- 10 contract files: subcontract-dev, subcontract-pm,
  subcontract-marketing, services-agreement-sloboda, nda-general
  — each in uk + en

Contract bodies contain: preamble with parties, key terms table,
numbered sections with <Clause> inlines, signatures block with
bank/VAT/address placeholders, Attachment A rate sheet.

Export pipeline
- scripts/build-exports.mjs now loads contracts collection
- inlineClauses() resolver replaces <Clause> tags with clause
  markdown at export time, stripping editorial commentary sections
  (Notes, Practical, Where used, etc.) via heading heuristic
- writes contract.{lang}.docx, contract.{lang}.md, contract.{lang}.mdx
  per contract, plus contract.zip bundling all language variants
- Generated 10 new .docx contracts + 5 zip bundles

Routes
- /contracts — index listing all compiled contracts
- /contracts/[id] — detail page with language toggle, big download
  buttons, full contract body rendered with <Clause> components
  resolved live
- Nav rail: added 'Контракти' as second link under 'Процеси'

ExportMenu extended with contractId prop + .docx (uk) and .docx (en)
as primary CTAs on contract pages.

Documents pages now have a 'Готовий до підпису контракт →' link
directing to the compiled contract.

Stats
- Pages:     47 → 57
- Clauses:   11 → 15 unique baseIds (30 variant files)
- Contracts: 0  → 5 unique baseIds (10 variant files)
- Broken wikilinks: 0

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: add client engagement pillar — MSA, NDA, SLA, client-onboarding, cases
body:
Second architectural milestone: the wiki now has two real pillars
instead of one. People/hiring (subcontractor onboarding, 3 flavors)
stays as-is; client engagement (MSA + NDA + SLA, client onboarding
workflow) is wholly new. First case binds both pillars into a
realistic scenario.

Pillar structure
----------------

- **people** (was the only pillar until today): Dev / PM / Marketing
  subcontractor onboarding
- **client-engagement** (new): client-onboarding workflow using
  NDA → MSA → SLA in sequence

Where they meet: during client-onboarding step 3 (SoW), capacity
planning may trigger a parallel subcontractor-onboarding workflow.
The first case documents exactly this chain.

New clauses (5 baseIds × uk+en = 10 files)
-------------------------------------------

- payment-dispute-client — flipped direction (Customer as payer),
  mirror of existing payment-dispute
- late-payments-interest — 0.5%/day on overdue invoices, unique
  to client contracts, one-directional
- client-ip-assignment — rolling IP transfer as payments are
  made, contrast to subcontract IP-strict
- confidentiality-mutual — core of NDA, extractable for other
  contracts later
- dispute-resolution-uk — the first genuinely cross-pillar
  shared clause: England & Wales + 60 days negotiation, identical
  in the Services Agreement AND all three subcontract docs

New documents (3)
-----------------

- services-agreement-sloboda-2026 — full client-facing MSA,
  composed of 4 new clauses + dispute-resolution-uk
- nda-general — standalone NDA, composed of confidentiality-mutual
  with the rest of the body inlined
- sla-template-sloboda — ops-level SLA referenced from MSA

New workflow
------------

- client-onboarding: qualify → discovery+NDA → SoW → MSA+SLA →
  kickoff, 5 steps across account-manager / head-of-delivery /
  legal / pm

New roles (2)
-------------

- account-manager — owns client relationship from first contact
  through signing and beyond
- head-of-delivery — confirms feasibility and staffing, can trigger
  subcontractor workflows

First case
----------

- new-eu-client-2026 — narrative that walks through a realistic EU
  fintech engagement: 3 weeks of client onboarding, parallel Dev and
  PM subcontractor onboarding for staffing, 6-month active project.
  Explicitly calls out that all 4 contracts in the case share the
  same dispute-resolution-uk clause.

Cross-pillar architectural proof
---------------------------------

Added `dispute-resolution-uk` to the composedOf of all three
existing subcontract documents. That clause is now genuinely
reused across 4 documents in 2 pillars. One file change →
four-document update. This is the first clause in the wiki that
demonstrates true cross-pillar sharing rather than intra-pillar
reuse.

Cases infrastructure
--------------------

- New route: src/pages/cases/[id].astro — detail page with TOC,
  workflow list, backlinks, related panel
- New route: src/pages/cases/index.astro — collection listing
- backlinks.ts: added forward-ref handling for the `workflows`
  field on case entries so backlinks from cases → workflows
  populate correctly

Home page
---------

- Hero copy now frames the wiki as two pillars meeting at cases
- Primary CTA jumps to the new-eu-client-2026 case instead of
  the first workflow
- Workflow list split into Client Engagement / People / Other
- Cases section added below

Plumbing
--------

- Added `aliases` field to the clauses schema — needed for natural
  wikilink references like [[Dispute Resolution UK]] resolving to
  the `dispute-resolution-uk` baseId
- Cases collection now actually used (was empty placeholder)
- Rail nav de-dimmed cases entry now that there's real content
- Case MDX uses quoted "2026" tag to satisfy Zod string-array
  schema

Stats
-----

- Pages:           33 → 47
- Clauses (base):   6 → 11
- Clause files:    12 → 22
- Documents:        3 → 6
- Workflows:        3 → 4
- Roles:            4 → 6
- Cases:            0 → 1
- Pagefind words: 1428 → 2976 (doubled)
- Broken wikilinks: 0

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: add PM + Marketing subcontract workflows
body:
Second real stress-test of the content model: three parallel
subcontract workflows (Developer, PM, Marketing) that share the
same backbone but use slightly different documents. Proves the
composition story with actual content, not just architecture.

New content
-----------

Workflows:
- subcontractor-onboarding-pm.mdx — Project Manager hiring
- subcontractor-onboarding-marketing.mdx — Marketing hiring, with
  a trial-brief step and notes on attribution / data access

Documents:
- subcontract-pm-2026.mdx — Sloboda Software GMBH PM subcontract,
  pulled from Drive. Composed of 5 clauses.
- subcontract-marketing-2026.mdx — ditto for marketing, EUR rates,
  digital/performance/strategic scope.

Clause variants:
- ip-assignment-services.uk.mdx
- ip-assignment-services.en.mdx

Why a second IP Assignment variant
----------------------------------

Reading the three subcontract templates side-by-side revealed
something interesting: Payment Dispute, Non-Solicitation,
Non-Compete, and Rate Increase are *byte-identical* across all
three. But the IP Assignment clause differs meaningfully:

- The Dev version carries an extended GPL/AGPL "onwards
  licensing" warning. That's defensive language against copyleft
  contamination of our codebase — a developer-specific risk.
- The PM and Marketing versions drop that paragraph and keep a
  simple "no open-source without written consent" line. PMs and
  marketers don't ship production code, so the contamination
  surface isn't there.

The content model handled this naturally: the Dev subcontract's
composedOf references `ip-assignment`, the PM and Marketing
subcontracts reference `ip-assignment-services`. Both clauses
have full uk/en variant pairs. Zero clause text duplication
between the PM and Marketing contracts.

This is the architectural point of the whole wiki: if Legal
changes the cap on Rate Increase from 10% to 12%, they edit
rate-increase.uk.mdx once and all three subcontracts update on
the next build. No copy-paste, no grep-and-replace.

Mechanical fixes
----------------

- content-index.mjs frontmatter parser only understood inline
  array syntax for `aliases: [...]`. The new docs initially used
  YAML block-list syntax, which silently lost the alias
  resolution. Converted both new docs to inline array form.
- Updated subcontractor-onboarding.mdx `related` list to point
  at the two new sibling workflows instead of hypothetical
  project-staffing / offboarding pages that don't exist yet.

Stats
-----

- Pages: 26 → 33
- Clauses: 5 → 6 (distinct baseIds)
- Clause variant files: 10 → 12
- Workflows: 1 → 3
- Documents: 1 → 3
- Broken wikilinks: 0 (all references resolve)

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: phase 2 — search, palette, walkthroughs, exports, graph, admin
body:
Massive UX pass bringing the wiki from "readable" to "navigable and
actionable." Everything ships together because the phases build on
each other.

## Navigation

- Fix: /workflows, /documents, /clauses, /terms, /roles now exist as
  actual index pages. The nav rail no longer 404s.
- Mobile hamburger + drawer at <=1023px, with the same links + search.
- Sticky rail on desktop, keyboard shortcut kbd on the search button.

## Search + command palette

- Pagefind 1.5 integration, indexed at build time across all pages.
  22 pages, ~1500 words, static and free.
- Cmd+K / Ctrl+K / "/" opens a full-screen modal palette (dialog
  element, native ::backdrop, lavender shadow offset).
- Palette has two modes: commands (jump, print, github) and search
  (Pagefind hits tagged by collection). Arrow keys + Enter navigate.
- Pagefind is lazy-loaded via dynamic import so the worker doesn't
  ship on initial page load.

## Reading UX

- On-this-page TOC in the right margin for workflow / document /
  clause pages. Scroll-spied via IntersectionObserver. Collapses
  inline on mobile.
- Anchor links on every h2/h3 via rehype-slug + rehype-autolink-
  headings. Paragraph-mark icon (¶) appears on heading hover,
  clickable for deep links.
- Dropcap treatment on the home hero paragraph using Fraunces
  display axis.
- Subtle display cursor blink on the home hero to replace the
  killed terminal prompt.
- Scroll progress bar (2px lavender) at the viewport top.
- Tooltip arrow pointer on wikilink popovers + mobile tap support
  (first tap = show tooltip, second = navigate).
- "Suggest edit on GitHub" and "View source" links in every page
  footer, pointing at the underlying MDX file.

## Content intelligence

- "Used in N places" pill on clause and term pages, breakdown by
  collection type, using the existing backlinks index.
- Related-by-tag panel below every page: scores all content by
  tag overlap, boosts same-collection matches, shows top 6.
- Tag / pillar / type filter chips on index pages, purely client-
  side.
- /changelog page generated from git log at build time. Each commit
  links to its GitHub URL and lists affected content files as
  clickable links.
- /admin content-health dashboard (public, per site policy) showing:
  broken wikilinks, orphans, drafts, missing updated timestamps,
  clauses without EN variants, expired reviews.
- reviewedBy / reviewedOn / reviewExpires fields on clauses and
  documents schemas, surfaced through /admin.

## Bilingual workflow — real this time

- Clauses split into per-language variant files:
  payment-dispute.uk.mdx + payment-dispute.en.mdx, etc.
- New schema fields: baseId (stable across languages) + lang
  (variant marker).
- clause-variants.ts resolver picks the best variant for a requested
  lang with fallback chain (uk → en → de).
- Language toggle component on clause and document pages. URL param
  ?lang=en switches the rendered variant at build time.
- Clause component (for inlining inside documents) now takes baseId
  instead of id, so document composition is language-aware.
- Backlinks + wikilink resolver collapse clause variants by baseId
  so [[Payment Dispute]] always resolves to a single canonical entry.
- Content-index.mjs dedupe uses baseId for clauses.

## Walkthrough mode

- New route: /workflows/[id]/walkthrough.
- Full-screen step-at-a-time guided mode for any workflow. Each
  step shows its title, role, and referenced documents.
- Keyboard navigation: arrows or j/k. Completion writes a
  localStorage marker.
- Progress dots at the top, big serif step titles, lavender side
  rail on referenced-docs card.
- Accessed via a "Walkthrough режим →" button on the normal
  workflow detail page.

## Export pipeline (phase 2 shipped)

- scripts/build-exports.mjs — build-time generator that runs via
  the `prebuild` npm hook.
- Generates under public/exports/ for every workflow, document, and
  clause:
    * source.mdx          raw MDX source
    * workflow.docx /
      document.docx /
      clause.docx         real Word doc via the `docx` library,
                          generated from a small markdown walker
                          (headings, paragraphs, bullets, numbered
                          lists, bold/italic, blockquotes, wikilinks
                          flattened to their display text)
    * bundle.zip /
      with-context.zip    via archiver, bundles the primary doc +
                          all referenced clauses/documents + a
                          generated README listing contents
- ExportMenu on every content page now shows real download links
  instead of placeholders.

## Graph view

- New /graph page with an SVG concentric-ring diagram.
- Workflows at center, documents nearby, clauses/terms/roles on
  outer rings. Each node is a clickable link to its page.
- Hovering a node highlights every edge connected to it in lavender.
- Legend at the top explains the color system.

## Plumbing

- rehype-slug + rehype-autolink-headings wired into both md and
  mdx integration pipelines.
- Astro ClientRouter stays enabled so tooltips, palette, scroll
  progress, mobile nav, walkthrough, and graph all re-hydrate on
  page-load via the astro:page-load event.
- Removed noisy cases-collection warnings via safe getCollection
  wrappers in backlinks.ts and admin.astro.

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: rebrand to editorial minimal — cream/black/lavender/peach
body:
Full visual switch from terminal-paper hybrid to editorial minimal,
matching the brand guide:

Palette
- Paper cream #F8F3E1 background
- Pure black #000 ink (softer greys for secondary text)
- Lavender #B8C2F5 as primary accent (calm state)
- Peach #F3B190 as hover-only accent (motion state)

Typography
- Fraunces Variable (serif) for all headings and body prose —
  closest free stand-in for Skema Pro Display with solid Cyrillic.
  Optical sizing and SOFT axis variation for display sizes.
- Raleway (sans) for all chrome: breadcrumbs, buttons, pills,
  metadata strips, nav rail, section dividers, table headers.
- Fluid type scale via clamp() — display scales 42px → 110px,
  h1 scales 32px → 60px.

Interaction vocabulary
- Links: black with half-opacity lavender underline at rest,
  full lavender + thicker underline on hover, 180ms color-only
  transition. Applied identically to body links, wikilinks, nav,
  backlinks, steps.
- Buttons: primary (white → peach on hover), secondary (lavender
  → white on hover), ghost (underline-only). Border-first,
  square corners, Raleway Bold uppercase label.
- Wikilinks: no more [[brackets]]. Inline with prose but type-
  tagged via data-wiki-type for tooltips.
- Tooltips: hard white card with black border and 6px lavender
  drop shadow, fade 180ms.

Layout
- Nav rail uses Raleway uppercase with lavender hover underline.
- Steps use 01 / 02 / 03 leading-zero numerals in Raleway Bold,
  hairline rule separators.
- Doc composition card softens to lavender border on hover.
- Section dividers are short Raleway uppercase labels above a
  hairline rule, not ASCII ornaments.

Motion
- Astro ClientRouter enabled for cross-page view transitions.
- Every interactive element uses the same 180ms color-only
  transition token (var(--t-base)). No scale, no translate.

Killed
- Terminal motifs: cursor blink, ASCII dividers, [[brackets]],
  monospace breadcrumbs, mono metadata blocks.
- Dark mode: brand guide doesn't define one; we commit to the
  single light palette.

Favicon updated to black serif D on cream with lavender underline.

author: workflowcat
subject: chore: add vercel.json and update .gitignore for .vercel

author: workflowcat
subject: feat: scaffold Drafters wiki with first workflow end-to-end
body:
Workflow wiki for IT services companies — bilingual, workflow-first,
modular. Astro 5 + MDX + content collections, static build for Vercel.

Content model (six collections, Zod-typed):
- workflows/  narrative pages binding everything together
- documents/  contracts composed of clauses
- clauses/    reusable legal fragments
- terms/      glossary entries with tooltips
- roles/      actors (Tech Lead, PM, Legal, IT Ops)
- cases/      scenario bundles (placeholder)

Core features:
- [[wikilinks]] resolved at build time via custom remark plugin,
  with tooltips hydrated from data attributes. Broken links flagged
  and logged as build warnings.
- Bidirectional backlinks auto-generated from both frontmatter refs
  and body wikilinks, rendered as grouped sections per page.
- Language variants declared at the clause/document level with
  fallback chain.
- Paper-meets-terminal design: warm cream + ink brown + amber accent,
  Source Serif 4 for body, JetBrains Mono for chrome (breadcrumbs,
  wikilinks, status pills, ASCII dividers). Dark mode included.
- Print stylesheet doubles as PDF source for future export pipeline.

First workflow populated end-to-end: Subcontractor onboarding
(developer). Pulls real clauses from Sloboda Software GMBH's 2026
developer subcontract template: Payment Dispute, Rate Increase,
IP Assignment, Non-solicitation, Non-compete.

License: MIT (code) / CC BY 4.0 (content, intended).
